summary: Fiery Scarlet>Fire, strong Althea and Sugar Magnolia on a high‑energy set
blurb: The show bursts with a scorching Scarlet > Fire transition and Jerry’s fiery Althea solo. Sugar Magnolia lands as a highlight, while the band rides tight grooves on Feel Like A Stranger and Black Peter. Overall performance is high‑energy despite a few rough spots.
review: On July 13, 1981 at Denver’s McNichols Sports Arena the Dead delivered a blistering summer night that still turns heads for its raw power. From the opening “Feel Like A Stranger” through an upbeat first set packed with “Althea,” “Jack‑A‑Roe,” and “Deal,” Jerry Garcia was in top form, ripping scorching riffs and delivering an expressive vocal on “Black Peter.” The second set hit a peak with the classic Scarlet > Fire segue – a crunchy, bass‑driven explosion that Phil Lesh drove forward while Brent’s keyboards added an otherworldly texture. A soaring “Sugar Magnolia” closed the night on a high note, and even the encore “(I Can’t Get No) Satisfaction” kept the momentum rolling. Multiple soundboard recordings capture the show with varying fidelity; the recommended SBD (gd81-07-13.sbd.lai.3903.sbefail.shnf) offers the clearest picture of the band’s fire, despite a bass‑heavy mix and occasional tape speed quirks. Fans will love the high‑octane energy, the standout jams, and the few rough edges that give this 1981 performance its authentic live charm.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are explicitly positive (highlighting Scarlet>Fire, Fire transition, and Sugar Magnolia) while the other two are mixed, noting both strong moments and equipment glitches. The consensus leans toward a solid, high‑energy performance with notable peaks, justifying a moderate positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Solid soundboard capture with clear overall mix; despite bass‑heavy tilt it preserves the energy of the Scarlet>Fire sequence and most of the set, making it the most complete and listenable version.

key_highlights: Scorching Scarlet > Fire transition that drives the second set
"Sugar Magnolia" stands out as a high‑octane showcase for Jerry
"Feel Like A Stranger" opener sets an upbeat tone for the night
Strong, emotive rendition of "Black Peter" despite occasional mix issues

band_performance.Jerry: Ripping, raunchy riffs on Althea and a scorching solo; loses his marbles on Sugar Magnolia delivering one of the best versions, with manic energy on El Paso despite occasional vocal flubs.
band_performance.Phil: Crunchy, powerful bass that drives the band; excellent dynamics on Scarlet>Fire and provides chord‑bomb lifts during the set, though the mix drops out at times.
band_performance.Bob: Offers rhythmic space for Jerry’s leads, especially on El Paso, contributing to the uptempo feel of the first set.
band_performance.Brent: Adds otherworldly texture with mellow touches, notably on It Must Have Been The Roses and throughout the jam sections.
band_performance.Mickey: Heavy, punchy drum sound with strong ride cymbal presence; high‑end distortion appears when volume is turned up but keeps solid pulse.
